tools_used: web search + fetch (Callais outcome, Trevino 25-918 status)
curl + pypdf (SCOTUS order-list PDF, fetch tool was 403'd)
helpful: statpack.json per-Term paid/IFP grant-rate split (5.4% paid anchor)
provisioned petition.txt full text — the QP and vehicle arguments drove the analysis
unrestricted forward-mode web retrieval — the Callais/Trevino context dominated the base rates
gaps: fedcourts query returned zero rows for both a topic filter (redistricting) and citation-overlap filters (Moore v. Harper / Covington / Alexander) — no cert-stage priors surfaced for a high-salience election-law petition; topic/citation coverage of the modern cert slice seems thin

the committed statpack lacks the 'Segment base rate by salience band' section the predict prompt tells cells to anchor on, and carries no called-for-response (CFR) cut — CFR-after-waiver was this cell's strongest docket signal and had no corpus base rate
supremecourt.gov blocks the engine's fetch tool (HTTP 403), so order lists/dockets need a curl+pdf-extraction workaround; a sanctioned SCOTUS-docket/order-list fetcher would remove that friction
the docket-document fetcher provisioned the petition but not the filed BIO (see flags.json)
notes: Second citation-filtered corpus query cost 418 ranged GETs / ~110 MB to return nothing; if that is typical for citation-overlap misses, a cheap negative-result short-circuit would save transfer.
